creator: feld@FreeBSD.org
text:
My Jenkins server has been kept up to date quite regularly and previous upgrades went without any problems. This morning I did a pkg upgrade to version 1.580 and it would not start properly; kept giving Jetty errors:

Error 503 Service Unavailable

I checked /var/log/jenkins.log and found a permissions error in /usr/local/jenkins. Unfortunately I don't have the exact line as it appears every time you restart Jenkins the log file is overwritten...

I noticed /usr/local/jenkins was owned by root:wheel. Changing it to jenkins:wheel and restarting jenkins again solved the problem.

I then looked at the directory listing and see the following two files which are owned by jenkins:wheel instead of jenkins:jenkins like the rest of the files

-rw-r--r--   1 jenkins  wheel     159 Sep 19 07:52 hudson.model.UpdateCenter.xml
-rw-r--r--   1 jenkins  wheel     907 Sep 19 07:52 nodeMonitors.xml

Perhaps these are the new files that needed to be written when starting up the service?

Ensuring JENKINS_HOME has jenkins:jenkins ownership should solve this problem.
